_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of Import/Export Regulations related to cryptography and other security technologies.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of data protection law, including GDPR, CCPA/CPRA, and sector-specific privacy regulations, with emphasis on the legal bases for processing, data subject rights, cross-border transfer mechanisms, and enforcement precedent.
comments: DCWF ksa superset of WIDAI SP domain. SP-K-007 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-K-002 (SP, cosine: 0.600), RC-K-007 (RC, cosine: 0.586).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.1/10 (raw 0.209). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge, regulations]. DCWF-only terms [cryptography, export, import, related, security, technologies] vs WIDAI-only terms [bases, border, ccpa, cpra, cross, data] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.867 (P=0.888, R=0.847). P exceeds R by 0.041 — the DCWF element's 11 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 29 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (99.8%). Despite 'Superset of' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Knowledge of Import/Export Regulations related to cryptography and other securit" and "Knowledge of data protection law, including GDPR, CCPA/CPRA, and sector-specific". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.657 > STS 0.209 (gap +0.448). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [knowledge, regulations] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 10.5%. Shared tokens: [knowledge, regulations]. DCWF-only: [cryptography, export, import, related, security, technologies]. WIDAI-only: [bases, border, ccpa, cpra, cross, data].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 99.8% on Superset of classification. The matched KSA operates in a different professional domain than the obligation/competency requires. STS 0.209 passed the threshold but secondary signals contradict the match.
STS 0.209 barely clears classification threshold for Superset of. This match is fragile and may indicate insufficient KSA coverage for this competency area.

Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.448 with low STS 0.209. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
